You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.4 (commit: 1f67fab3).

## Issue to Resolve

Update RELEASE-NOTES
`[***]  Added plan selection, domain selection, and checkout screens in site creation flow (...)`

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `RELEASE-NOTES.txt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/sitecreation/SiteCreationMainVM.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/sitecreation/previews/SitePreviewViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/sitecreation/progress/SiteCreationProgressViewModel.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 23→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.DomainsRegistrationTracker

 25→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationMainVM.SiteCreationScreenTitle.ScreenTitleEmpty
 26→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationMainVM.SiteCreationScreenTitle.ScreenTitleGeneral
 27→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationMainVM.SiteCreationScreenTitle.ScreenTitleStepCount
 28→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Completed
 29→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Created
 30→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.CreatedButNotFetched
 31→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.NotCreated
 32→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.domains.DomainModel
 33→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationSource
 34→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationTracker
 35→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.plans.PlanModel
 36→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.usecases.FetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase
 37→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString.UiStringRes
 38→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 39→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 40→import org.wordpress.android.util.NetworkUtilsWrapper
 41→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.getParcelableCompat
 42→import org.wordpress.android.util.image.ImageManager
 43→import org.wordpress.android.util.wizard.WizardManager
 44→import org.wordpress.android.util.wizard.WizardNavigationTarget
 45→import org.wordpress.android.util.wizard.WizardState
 46→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
 47→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.SingleEventObservable
 48→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.SingleLiveEvent
 49→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.helpers.DialogHolder
 50→import javax.inject.Inject
 51→
 52→const val TAG_WARNING_DIALOG = "back_pressed_warning_dialog"
 53→const val KEY_CURRENT_STEP = "key_current_step"
 54→const val KEY_SITE_CREATION_STATE = "key_site_creation_state"
 55→
 56→@Parcelize
 57→data class SiteCreationState(
 58→    val siteIntent: String? = null,
 59→    val siteName: String? = null,
 60→    val segmentId: Long? = null,
 61→    val siteDesign: String? = null,
 62→    val domain: DomainModel? = null,
 63→    val plan: PlanModel? = null,
 64→    val result: SiteCreationResult = NotCreated,
 65→) : WizardState, Parcelable
 66→
 67→typealias NavigationTarget = WizardNavigationTarget<SiteCreationStep, SiteCreationState>
 68→
 69→sealed interface SiteCreationResult : Parcelable {
 70→    @Parcelize
 71→    object NotCreated : SiteCreationResult
 72→
 73→    sealed interface Created: SiteCreationResult {
 74→        val site: SiteModel
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    sealed interface CreatedButNotFetched : Created {
 78→        @Parcelize
 79→        data class NotInLocalDb(
 80→            override val site: SiteModel,
 81→        ) : CreatedButNotFetched
 82→
 83→        @Parcelize
 84→        data class InCart(
 85→            override val site: SiteModel,
 86→        ) : CreatedButNotFetched
 87→
 88→        @Parcelize
 89→        data class DomainRegistrationPurchased(
 90→            val domainName: String,
 91→            val email: String,
 92→            override val site: SiteModel,
 93→        ) : CreatedButNotFetched
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    @Parcelize
 97→    data class Completed(
 98→        override val site: SiteModel,
 99→    ) : Created
100→}
101→
102→typealias SiteCreationCompletionEvent = Pair<SiteCreationResult, Boolean>
103→
104→@HiltViewModel
105→class SiteCreationMainVM @Inject constructor(
106→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
107→    private val wizardManager: WizardManager<SiteCreationStep>,
108→    private val networkUtils: NetworkUtilsWrapper,
109→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
110→    private val fetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase: FetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase,
111→    private val imageManager: ImageManager,
112→    private val jetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil: JetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil,
113→    private val domainsRegistrationTracker: DomainsRegistrationTracker,
114→) : ViewModel() {
115→    init {
116→        dispatcher.register(fetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase)
117→    }
118→
119→    override fun onCleared() {
120→        super.onCleared()
121→        dispatcher.unregister(fetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase)
122→    }
123→
124→    var siteCreationDisabled: Boolean = false
125→    private var isStarted = false
126→
127→    private lateinit var siteCreationState: SiteCreationState
128→
129→    internal var preloadingJob: Job? = null
130→
131→    val navigationTargetObservable: SingleEventObservable<NavigationTarget> by lazy {
132→        SingleEventObservable(
133→            wizardManager.navigatorLiveData.map {
134→                clearOldSiteCreationState(it)
135→                WizardNavigationTarget(it, siteCreationState)
136→            }
137→        )
138→    }
139→
140→    private val _dialogAction = SingleLiveEvent<DialogHolder>()
141→    val dialogActionObservable: LiveData<DialogHolder> = _dialogAction
142→
143→    private val _onCompleted = SingleLiveEvent<SiteCreationCompletionEvent>()
144→    val onCompleted: LiveData<SiteCreationCompletionEvent> = _onCompleted
145→
146→    private val _exitFlowObservable = SingleLiveEvent<Unit?>()
147→    val exitFlowObservable: LiveData<Unit?> = _exitFlowObservable
148→
149→    private val _onBackPressedObservable = SingleLiveEvent<Unit?>()
150→    val onBackPressedObservable: LiveData<Unit?> = _onBackPressedObservable
151→
152→    private val _showJetpackOverlay = MutableLiveData<Event<Boolean>>()
153→    val showJetpackOverlay: LiveData<Event<Boolean>> = _showJetpackOverlay
154→
155→    private val _showDomainCheckout = SingleLiveEvent<CheckoutDetails>()
156→    val showDomainCheckout: LiveData<CheckoutDetails> = _showDomainCheckout
157→
158→    fun start(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, siteCreationSource: SiteCreationSource) {
159→        if (isStarted) return
160→        if (savedInstanceState == null) {
161→            tracker.trackSiteCreationAccessed(siteCreationSource)
162→            siteCreationState = SiteCreationState()
163→            if (jetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil.shouldShowSiteCreationOverlay())
164→                showJetpackOverlay()
165→            if (jetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil.shouldDisableSiteCreation())
166→                siteCreationDisabled = true
167→            else
168→                showSiteCreationNextStep()
169→        } else {
170→            siteCreationState = requireNotNull(savedInstanceState.getParcelableCompat(KEY_SITE_CREATION_STATE))
171→            val currentStepIndex = savedInstanceState.getInt(KEY_CURRENT_STEP)
172→            wizardManager.setCurrentStepIndex(currentStepIndex)
173→        }
174→        isStarted = true
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun showSiteCreationNextStep() {
178→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun showJetpackOverlay() {
182→        _showJetpackOverlay.value = Event(true)
183→    }
184→
185→    fun preloadThumbnails(context: Context) {
186→        if (preloadingJob == null) {
187→            preloadingJob = viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
188→                if (networkUtils.isNetworkAvailable()) {
189→                    val response = fetchHomePageLayoutsUseCase.fetchStarterDesigns()
190→
191→                    // Else clause added to better understand the reason that causes this crash:
192→                    // https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/issues/17020
193→                    if (response.isError) {
194→                        AppLog.e(T.THEMES, "Error preloading starter designs: ${response.error}")
195→                        return@launch
196→                    } else if (response.designs == null) {
197→                        AppLog.e(T.THEMES, "Null starter designs response: $response")
198→                        return@launch
199→                    }
200→
201→                    for (design in response.designs) {
202→                        imageManager.preload(context, MShot(design.previewMobile))
203→                    }
204→                }
205→                preloadingJob = null
206→            }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    fun writeToBundle(outState: Bundle) {
211→        outState.putInt(KEY_CURRENT_STEP, wizardManager.currentStep)
212→        outState.putParcelable(KEY_SITE_CREATION_STATE, siteCreationState)
213→    }
214→
215→    fun onSiteIntentSelected(intent: String?) {
216→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(siteIntent = intent)
217→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
218→    }
219→
220→    fun onSiteIntentSkipped() {
221→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(siteIntent = null)
222→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
223→    }
224→
225→    fun onSiteNameSkipped() {
226→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(siteName = null)
227→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
228→    }
229→
230→    fun onSiteNameEntered(siteName: String) {
231→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(siteName = siteName)
232→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
233→    }
234→
235→    fun onDesignSelected(siteDesign: String) {
236→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(siteDesign = siteDesign)
237→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
238→    }
239→
240→    fun onBackPressed() {
241→        return if (wizardManager.isLastStep()) {
242→            if (siteCreationState.result is Completed) {
243→                exitFlow(false)
244→            } else {
245→                _dialogAction.value = DialogHolder(
246→                    tag = TAG_WARNING_DIALOG,
247→                    title = null,
248→                    message = UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_preview_back_pressed_warning),
249→                    positiveButton = UiStringRes(R.string.exit),
250→                    negativeButton = UiStringRes(R.string.cancel)
251→                )
252→            }
253→        } else {
254→            wizardManager.onBackPressed()
255→            _onBackPressedObservable.call()
256→        }
257→    }
258→
259→    private fun clearOldSiteCreationState(wizardStep: SiteCreationStep) {
260→        if (wizardStep == SiteCreationStep.DOMAINS) {
261→            siteCreationState.domain?.let {
262→                siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(domain = null)
263→            }
264→        }
265→        if (wizardStep == SiteCreationStep.PLANS) {
266→            siteCreationState.plan?.let {
267→                siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(plan = null)
268→            }
269→        }
270→    }
271→
272→    fun onDomainsScreenFinished(domain: DomainModel) {
273→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(domain = domain)
274→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
275→    }
276→
277→    fun onPlanSelection(plan: PlanModel) {
278→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(plan = plan)
279→        if (plan.productSlug == "free_plan") {
280→            // if they select a paid domain, then choose a free plan, with free domain on plan selection screen
281→            siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(
282→                domain = DomainModel(
283→                    domainName = plan.productName.orEmpty(),
284→                    isFree = true,
285→                    cost = "",
286→                    productId = 0,
287→                    supportsPrivacy = false
288→                )
289→            )
290→        }
291→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
292→    }
293→
294→    fun screenTitleForWizardStep(step: SiteCreationStep): SiteCreationScreenTitle {
295→        val stepPosition = wizardManager.stepPosition(step)
296→        val stepCount = wizardManager.stepsCount
297→        val firstStep = stepPosition == 1
298→        val lastStep = stepPosition == stepCount
299→        val singleInBetweenStepDomains = step.name == SiteCreationStep.DOMAINS.name
300→
301→        return when {
302→            firstStep -> ScreenTitleGeneral(R.string.new_site_creation_screen_title_general)
303→            lastStep -> ScreenTitleEmpty
304→            singleInBetweenStepDomains -> ScreenTitleGeneral(R.string.new_site_creation_domain_header_title)
305→            else -> ScreenTitleStepCount(
306→                R.string.new_site_creation_screen_title_step_count,
307→                stepCount - 2, // -2 -> first = general title (Create Site), last item = empty title
308→                stepPosition - 1 // -1 -> first item has general title - Create Site
309→            )
310→        }
311→    }
312→
313→    fun onCartCreated(checkoutDetails: CheckoutDetails) {
314→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(result = CreatedButNotFetched.InCart(checkoutDetails.site))
315→        domainsRegistrationTracker.trackDomainsPurchaseWebviewViewed(checkoutDetails.site, isSiteCreation = true)
316→        _showDomainCheckout.value = checkoutDetails
317→    }
318→
319→    fun onCheckoutResult(event: DomainRegistrationCompletedEvent?) {
320→        if (event == null) return onBackPressed()
321→        domainsRegistrationTracker.trackDomainsPurchaseDomainSuccess(isSiteCreation = true)
322→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.run {
323→            check(result is CreatedButNotFetched.InCart)
324→            copy(
325→                result = CreatedButNotFetched.DomainRegistrationPurchased(
326→                    event.domainName,
327→                    event.email,
328→                    result.site,
329→                )
330→            )
331→        }
332→        wizardManager.showNextStep()
333→    }
334→
335→    fun onFreeSiteCreated(site: SiteModel) {
336→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(result = CreatedButNotFetched.NotInLocalDb(site))
337→        if (checkNotNull(siteCreationState.domain).isFree) {
338→            wizardManager.showNextStep()
339→        }
340→    }
341→
342→    fun onWizardCancelled() {
343→        _onCompleted.value = NotCreated to isSiteTitleTaskCompleted()
344→    }
345→
346→    fun onWizardFinished(result: Created) {
347→        siteCreationState = siteCreationState.copy(result = result)
348→        _onCompleted.value = result to isSiteTitleTaskCompleted()
349→    }
350→
351→    private fun isSiteTitleTaskCompleted() = !siteCreationState.siteName.isNullOrBlank()
352→
353→    /**
354→     * Exits the flow and tracks an event when the user force-exits the "site creation in progress" before it completes.
355→     */
356→    private fun exitFlow(forceExit: Boolean) {
357→        if (forceExit) {
358→            tracker.trackFlowExited()
359→        }
360→        _exitFlowObservable.call()
361→    }
362→
363→    fun onPositiveDialogButtonClicked(instanceTag: String) {
364→        check(instanceTag == TAG_WARNING_DIALOG) { "Unknown dialog tag: $instanceTag" }
365→        exitFlow(true)
366→    }
367→
368→    fun onNegativeDialogButtonClicked(instanceTag: String) {
369→        check(instanceTag == TAG_WARNING_DIALOG) { "Unknown dialog tag: $instanceTag" }
370→        // do nothing
371→    }
372→
373→    sealed class SiteCreationScreenTitle {
374→        data class ScreenTitleStepCount(@StringRes val resId: Int, val stepsCount: Int, val stepPosition: Int) :
375→            SiteCreationScreenTitle()
376→
377→        data class ScreenTitleGeneral(@StringRes val resId: Int) :
378→            SiteCreationScreenTitle()
379→
380→        object ScreenTitleEmpty : SiteCreationScreenTitle() {
381→            const val screenTitle = ""
382→        }
383→    }
384→}
385→

 33→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 34→import org.wordpress.android.util.UrlUtilsWrapper
 35→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.SingleLiveEvent
 36→import javax.inject.Inject
 37→import javax.inject.Named
 38→import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 39→
 40→@HiltViewModel
 41→class SitePreviewViewModel @Inject constructor(
 42→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 43→    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
 44→    private val fetchWpComSiteUseCase: FetchWpComSiteUseCase,
 45→    private val urlUtils: UrlUtilsWrapper,
 46→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 47→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 48→    @Named(UI_THREAD) private val mainDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 49→) : ViewModel(), CoroutineScope {
 50→    init {
 51→        dispatcher.register(fetchWpComSiteUseCase)
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun onCleared() {
 55→        super.onCleared()
 56→        dispatcher.unregister(fetchWpComSiteUseCase)
 57→        job.cancel()
 58→    }
 59→
 60→    private val job = Job()
 61→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 62→        get() = bgDispatcher + job
 63→    private var isStarted = false
 64→    private var webviewFullyLoadedTracked = false
 65→
 66→    private var siteDesign: String? = null
 67→    private var isFree: Boolean = true
 68→
 69→    private lateinit var result: Created
 70→    private lateinit var domainName: String
 71→
 72→    private val _uiState: MutableLiveData<SitePreviewUiState> = MutableLiveData()
 73→    val uiState: LiveData<SitePreviewUiState> = _uiState
 74→
 75→    private val _preloadPreview: MutableLiveData<String> = MutableLiveData()
 76→    val preloadPreview: LiveData<String> = _preloadPreview
 77→
 78→    private val _onOkButtonClicked = SingleLiveEvent<Created>()
 79→    val onOkButtonClicked: LiveData<Created> = _onOkButtonClicked
 80→
 81→    fun start(siteCreationState: SiteCreationState) {
 82→        if (isStarted) return else isStarted = true
 83→        require(siteCreationState.result is Created)
 84→        siteDesign = siteCreationState.siteDesign
 85→        result = siteCreationState.result
 86→        isFree = requireNotNull(siteCreationState.domain).isFree
 87→        domainName = requireNotNull(siteCreationState.domain).domainName
 88→        startPreLoadingWebView()
 89→        if (result is CreatedButNotFetched) {
 90→            launch {
 91→                fetchNewlyCreatedSiteModel(result.site.siteId)?.let {
 92→                    result = Completed(it)
 93→                }
 94→            }
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    fun onOkButtonClicked() {
 99→        tracker.trackPreviewOkButtonTapped()
100→        _onOkButtonClicked.postValue(result)
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun startPreLoadingWebView() {
104→        tracker.trackPreviewLoading(siteDesign)
105→        launch {
106→            /**
107→             * If the webview is still not loaded after some delay, we'll show the loading shimmer animation instead
108→             * of the full screen progress, so the user is not blocked for taking actions.
109→             */
110→            withContext(mainDispatcher) {
111→                if (uiState.value !is SitePreviewContentUiState) {
112→                    tracker.trackPreviewWebviewShown(siteDesign)
113→                    updateUiState(SitePreviewLoadingShimmerState(isFree, createSitePreviewData()))
114→                }
115→            }
116→        }
117→        // Load the newly created site in the webview
118→        result.site.url?.let { url ->
119→            val urlToLoad = urlUtils.addUrlSchemeIfNeeded(
120→                url = url,
121→                addHttps = isWordPressComSubDomain(url)
122→            )
123→            AppLog.v(T.SITE_CREATION, "Site preview will load for url: $urlToLoad")
124→            _preloadPreview.postValue(urlToLoad)
125→        }
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Fetch newly created site model - supports retry with linear backoff.
130→     */
131→    private suspend fun fetchNewlyCreatedSiteModel(remoteSiteId: Long): SiteModel? {
132→        val onSiteFetched = fetchWpComSiteUseCase.fetchSiteWithRetry(remoteSiteId)
133→        return if (!onSiteFetched.isError) {
134→            return requireNotNull(siteStore.getSiteBySiteId(remoteSiteId)) {
135→                "Site successfully fetched but has not been found in the local db."
136→            }
137→        } else {
138→            null
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun onUrlLoaded() {
143→        if (!webviewFullyLoadedTracked) {
144→            webviewFullyLoadedTracked = true
145→            tracker.trackPreviewWebviewFullyLoaded(siteDesign)
146→        }
147→        /**
148→         * Update the ui state if the loading or error screen is being shown.
149→         * In other words don't update it after a configuration change.
150→         */
151→        if (uiState.value !is SitePreviewContentUiState) {
152→            updateUiState(SitePreviewContentUiState(isFree, createSitePreviewData()))
153→        }
154→    }
155→
156→    fun onWebViewError() {
157→        if (uiState.value !is SitePreviewWebErrorUiState) {
158→            updateUiState(SitePreviewWebErrorUiState(isFree, createSitePreviewData()))
159→        }
160→    }
161→
162→    private fun createSitePreviewData(): UrlData {
163→        val url = domainName
164→        val subDomain = urlUtils.extractSubDomain(url)
165→        val fullUrl = urlUtils.addUrlSchemeIfNeeded(url, true)
166→        val subDomainIndices = 0 to subDomain.length
167→        val domainIndices = subDomainIndices.second.coerceAtMost(url.length) to url.length
168→        return UrlData(
169→            fullUrl,
170→            url,
171→            subDomainIndices,
172→            domainIndices
173→        )
174→    }
175→
176→    private fun updateUiState(uiState: SitePreviewUiState) {
177→        _uiState.value = uiState
178→    }
179→
180→    sealed class SitePreviewUiState(
181→        open val urlData: UrlData,
182→        val webViewVisibility: Boolean = false,
183→        val webViewErrorVisibility: Boolean = false,
184→        val shimmerVisibility: Boolean = false,
185→        val subtitle: UiString,
186→        val caption: UiString?,
187→    ) {
188→        data class SitePreviewContentUiState(
189→            val isFree: Boolean,
190→            override val urlData: UrlData,
191→        ) : SitePreviewUiState(
192→            urlData = urlData,
193→            webViewVisibility = true,
194→            webViewErrorVisibility = false,
195→            subtitle = getSubtitle(isFree),
196→            caption = getCaption(isFree),
197→        )
198→
199→        data class SitePreviewWebErrorUiState(
200→            val isFree: Boolean,
201→            override val urlData: UrlData,
202→        ) : SitePreviewUiState(
203→            urlData = urlData,
204→            webViewVisibility = false,
205→            webViewErrorVisibility = true,
206→            subtitle = getSubtitle(isFree),
207→            caption = getCaption(isFree),
208→        )
209→
210→        data class SitePreviewLoadingShimmerState(
211→            val isFree: Boolean,
212→            override val urlData: UrlData,
213→        ) : SitePreviewUiState(
214→            urlData = urlData,
215→            shimmerVisibility = true,
216→            subtitle = getSubtitle(isFree),
217→            caption = getCaption(isFree),
218→        )
219→
220→        companion object {
221→            private fun getSubtitle(isFree: Boolean): UiString {
222→                return if (isFree) {
223→                    UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_preview_subtitle)
224→                } else {
225→                    UiStringResWithParams(
226→                        R.string.new_site_creation_preview_subtitle_paid,
227→                        UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_preview_subtitle),
228→                    )
229→                }
230→            }
231→
232→            private fun getCaption(isFree: Boolean): UiStringRes? {
233→                return UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_preview_caption_paid).takeIf { !isFree }
234→            }
235→        }
236→
237→        data class UrlData(
238→            val fullUrl: String,
239→            val shortUrl: String,
240→            val domainIndices: Pair<Int, Int>,
241→            val subDomainIndices: Pair<Int, Int>
242→        )
243→    }
244→}

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.progress
  2→
  3→import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
  4→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
  5→import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
  6→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
  7→import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
  8→import kotlinx.coroutines.delay
  9→import org.greenrobot.eventbus.Subscribe
 10→import org.greenrobot.eventbus.ThreadMode
 11→import org.wordpress.android.R
 12→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 13→import org.wordpress.android.modules.UI_THREAD
 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.DomainRegistrationCheckoutWebViewActivity.OpenCheckout.CheckoutDetails
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.usecases.CreateCartUseCase
 16→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Completed
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Created
 18→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.CreatedButNotFetched.InCart
 19→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationState
 20→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.domains.DomainModel
 21→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationErrorType.INTERNET_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR
 22→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationErrorType.UNKNOWN
 23→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationTracker
 24→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.progress.SiteCreationProgressViewModel.SiteProgressUiState.Error.CartError
 25→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.progress.SiteCreationProgressViewModel.SiteProgressUiState.Error.ConnectionError
 26→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.progress.SiteCreationProgressViewModel.SiteProgressUiState.Error.GenericError
 27→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.progress.SiteCreationProgressViewModel.SiteProgressUiState.Loading
 28→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceData
 29→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState
 30→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.CREATE_SITE
 31→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.FAILURE
 32→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.IDLE
 33→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.SUCCESS
 34→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString
 35→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString.UiStringRes
 36→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 37→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 38→import org.wordpress.android.util.NetworkUtilsWrapper
 39→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ScopedViewModel
 40→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.SingleLiveEvent
 41→import javax.inject.Inject
 42→import javax.inject.Named
 43→
 44→private const val CONNECTION_ERROR_DELAY_TO_SHOW_LOADING_STATE = 1000L
 45→const val LOADING_STATE_TEXT_ANIMATION_DELAY = 2000L
 46→private const val ERROR_CONTEXT = "site_preview"
 47→
 48→private val loadingTexts = listOf(
 49→    UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_creating_site_loading_1),
 50→    UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_creating_site_loading_2),
 51→    UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_creating_site_loading_3),
 52→    UiStringRes(R.string.new_site_creation_creating_site_loading_4)
 53→)
 54→
 55→@HiltViewModel
 56→class SiteCreationProgressViewModel @Inject constructor(
 57→    private val networkUtils: NetworkUtilsWrapper,
 58→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 59→    private val createCartUseCase: CreateCartUseCase,
 60→    @Named(UI_THREAD) mainDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 61→) : ScopedViewModel(mainDispatcher) {
 62→    private var loadingAnimationJob: Job? = null
 63→
 64→    private lateinit var siteCreationState: SiteCreationState
 65→    private lateinit var domain: DomainModel
 66→    private lateinit var site: SiteModel
 67→
 68→    private var lastReceivedServiceState: SiteCreationServiceState? = null
 69→    private var serviceStateForRetry: SiteCreationServiceState? = null
 70→
 71→    private val _uiState: MutableLiveData<SiteProgressUiState> = MutableLiveData()
 72→    val uiState: LiveData<SiteProgressUiState> = _uiState
 73→
 74→    private val _startCreateSiteService: SingleLiveEvent<StartServiceData> = SingleLiveEvent()
 75→    val startCreateSiteService: LiveData<StartServiceData> = _startCreateSiteService
 76→
 77→    private val _onHelpClicked = SingleLiveEvent<Unit?>()
 78→    val onHelpClicked: LiveData<Unit?> = _onHelpClicked
 79→
 80→    private val _onCancelWizardClicked = SingleLiveEvent<Unit?>()
 81→    val onCancelWizardClicked: LiveData<Unit?> = _onCancelWizardClicked
 82→
 83→    private val _onFreeSiteCreated = SingleLiveEvent<SiteModel>()
 84→    val onFreeSiteCreated: LiveData<SiteModel> = _onFreeSiteCreated
 85→
 86→    private val _onCartCreated = SingleLiveEvent<CheckoutDetails>()
 87→    val onCartCreated: LiveData<CheckoutDetails> = _onCartCreated
 88→
 89→    override fun onCleared() {
 90→        super.onCleared()
 91→        loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
 92→        createCartUseCase.clear()
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun start(siteCreationState: SiteCreationState) {
 96→        if (siteCreationState.result is Created) {
 97→            check(siteCreationState.result !is Completed) { "Unexpected state on progress screen." }
 98→            // reuse the previously blog when returning with the same domain
 99→            if (siteCreationState.domain == domain) {
100→                site = siteCreationState.result.site
101→                if (siteCreationState.result is InCart) {
102→                    createCart()
103→                }
104→                return
105→            }
106→        }
107→        this.siteCreationState = siteCreationState
108→        domain = requireNotNull(siteCreationState.domain) { "domain required to create a site" }
109→
110→        runLoadingAnimationUi()
111→        startCreateSiteService()
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun startCreateSiteService(previousState: SiteCreationServiceState? = null) {
115→        if (networkUtils.isNetworkAvailable()) {
116→            siteCreationState.let { state ->
117→                // A non-null [segmentId] may invalidate the [siteDesign] selection
118→                // https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/issues/13749
119→                val segmentIdentifier = state.segmentId.takeIf { state.siteDesign != null }
120→                val serviceData = SiteCreationServiceData(
121→                    segmentIdentifier,
122→                    state.siteDesign,
123→                    domain.domainName,
124→                    state.siteName,
125→                    domain.isFree,
126→                )
127→                _startCreateSiteService.value = StartServiceData(serviceData, previousState)
128→            }
129→        } else {
130→            showFullscreenErrorWithDelay()
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    fun retry() {
135→        when (uiState.value) {
136→            is GenericError,
137→            is ConnectionError -> startCreateSiteService(serviceStateForRetry)
138→
139→            is CartError -> createCart()
140→            else -> error("Unexpected state for retry: ${uiState.value}")
141→        }
142→        runLoadingAnimationUi()
143→    }
144→
145→    fun onHelpClicked() = _onHelpClicked.call()
146→
147→    fun onCancelWizardClicked() = _onCancelWizardClicked.call()
148→
149→    private fun showFullscreenErrorWithDelay() {
150→        runLoadingAnimationUi()
151→        launch {
152→            // We show the loading indicator for a bit so the user has some feedback when they press retry
153→            delay(CONNECTION_ERROR_DELAY_TO_SHOW_LOADING_STATE)
154→            tracker.trackErrorShown(ERROR_CONTEXT, INTERNET_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR)
155→            updateUiState(ConnectionError)
156→        }
157→    }
158→
159→    /**
160→     * The service automatically shows system notifications when site creation is in progress and the app is in
161→     * the background. We need to connect to the `AutoForeground` service from the View(Fragment), as only the View
162→     * knows when the app is in the background. Required parameter for `ServiceEventConnection` is also
163→     * the observer/listener of the `SiteCreationServiceState` (VM in our case), therefore we can't simply register
164→     * to the EventBus from the ViewModel and we have to use `sticky` events instead.
165→     */
166→    @Subscribe(threadMode = ThreadMode.BACKGROUND, sticky = true)
167→    @Suppress("unused")
168→    fun onSiteCreationServiceStateUpdated(event: SiteCreationServiceState) {
169→        if (lastReceivedServiceState == event) return // filter out events which we've already received
170→        lastReceivedServiceState = event
171→        when (event.step) {
172→            IDLE, CREATE_SITE -> Unit
173→            SUCCESS -> {
174→                site = mapPayloadToSiteModel(event.payload)
175→                _onFreeSiteCreated.postValue(site) // MainVM will navigate forward if the domain is free
176→                if (!domain.isFree && siteCreationState.plan?.productId != 0) {
177→                    createCart()
178→                }
179→            }
180→
181→            FAILURE -> {
182→                serviceStateForRetry = event.payload as SiteCreationServiceState
183→                tracker.trackErrorShown(
184→                    ERROR_CONTEXT,
185→                    UNKNOWN,
186→                    "SiteCreation service failed"
187→                )
188→                updateUiStateAsync(GenericError)
189→            }
190→        }
191→    }
192→
193→    private fun mapPayloadToSiteModel(payload: Any?): SiteModel {
194→        require(payload is Pair<*, *>) { "Expected Pair in Payload, got: $payload" }
195→        val (blogId, blogUrl) = payload
196→        require(blogId is Long) { "Expected the 1st element in the Payload Pair to be a Long, got: $blogId" }
197→        require(blogUrl is String) { "Expected the 2nd element in the Payload Pair to be a Long, got: $blogUrl" }
198→        return SiteModel().apply { siteId = blogId; url = blogUrl }
199→    }
200→
201→    private fun createCart() = launch {
202→        AppLog.d(T.SITE_CREATION, "Creating cart: $domain")
203→
204→        val event = createCartUseCase.execute(
205→            site,
206→            domain.productId,
207→            domain.domainName,
208→            domain.supportsPrivacy,
209→            false,
210→            planProductId = siteCreationState.plan?.productId
211→        )
212→
213→        if (event.isError) {
214→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "Failed cart creation: ${event.error.message}")
215→            updateUiStateAsync(CartError)
216→        } else {
217→            AppLog.d(T.SITE_CREATION, "Successful cart creation: ${event.cartDetails}")
218→            _onCartCreated.postValue(CheckoutDetails(site, domain.domainName))
219→        }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun runLoadingAnimationUi() {
223→        loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
224→        loadingAnimationJob = launch {
225→            loadingTexts.forEachIndexed { i, uiString ->
226→                updateUiState(
227→                    Loading(
228→                        animate = i != 0, // the first text should appear without an animation
229→                        loadingTextResId = uiString
230→                    )
231→                )
232→                delay(LOADING_STATE_TEXT_ANIMATION_DELAY)
233→            }
234→        }
235→    }
236→
237→    private fun updateUiState(uiState: SiteProgressUiState) {
238→        if (uiState !is Loading) loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
239→        _uiState.value = uiState
240→    }
241→
242→    private fun updateUiStateAsync(uiState: SiteProgressUiState) {
243→        if (uiState !is Loading) loadingAnimationJob?.cancel()
244→        _uiState.postValue(uiState)
245→    }
246→
247→    sealed class SiteProgressUiState(
248→        val progressLayoutVisibility: Boolean = false,
249→        val errorLayoutVisibility: Boolean = false
250→    ) {
251→        data class Loading(val loadingTextResId: UiString, val animate: Boolean) :
252→            SiteProgressUiState(progressLayoutVisibility = true)
253→
254→        sealed class Error constructor(
255→            val titleResId: Int,
256→            val subtitleResId: Int? = null,
257→            val showContactSupport: Boolean = false,
258→            val showCancelWizardButton: Boolean = true
259→        ) : SiteProgressUiState(errorLayoutVisibility = true) {
260→            object GenericError : Error(
261→                R.string.site_creation_error_generic_title,
262→                R.string.site_creation_error_generic_subtitle,
263→                showContactSupport = true
264→            )
265→
266→            object CartError : Error(
267→                R.string.site_creation_error_generic_title,
268→                R.string.site_creation_error_cart_subtitle,
269→                showContactSupport = true
270→            )
271→
272→            object ConnectionError : Error(
273→                R.string.no_network_message
274→            )
275→        }
276→    }
277→
278→    data class StartServiceData(
279→        val serviceData: SiteCreationServiceData,
280→        val previousState: SiteCreationServiceState?
281→    )
282→}

 64→import org.wordpress.android.util.config.SiteNameFeatureConfig
 65→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.exhaustive
 66→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.onBackPressedCompat
 67→import org.wordpress.android.util.wizard.WizardNavigationTarget
 68→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.observeEvent
 69→import javax.inject.Inject
 70→import android.R as AndroidR
 71→
 72→@AndroidEntryPoint
 73→class SiteCreationActivity : LocaleAwareActivity(),
 74→    IntentsScreenListener,
 75→    SiteNameScreenListener,
 76→    DomainsScreenListener,
 77→    PlansScreenListener,
 78→    OnHelpClickedListener,
 79→    BasicDialogPositiveClickInterface,
 80→    BasicDialogNegativeClickInterface {
 81→    @Inject
 82→    internal lateinit var uiHelpers: UiHelpers
 83→
 84→    @Inject
 85→    internal lateinit var siteNameFeatureConfig: SiteNameFeatureConfig
 86→    private val mainViewModel: SiteCreationMainVM by viewModels()
 87→    private val hppViewModel: HomePagePickerViewModel by viewModels()
 88→    private val siteCreationIntentsViewModel: SiteCreationIntentsViewModel by viewModels()
 89→    private val siteCreationSiteNameViewModel: SiteCreationSiteNameViewModel by viewModels()
 90→    private val jetpackFullScreenViewModel: JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayViewModel by viewModels()
 91→    private val progressViewModel: SiteCreationProgressViewModel by viewModels()
 92→    private val previewViewModel: SitePreviewViewModel by viewModels()
 93→
 94→    @Inject
 95→    internal lateinit var jetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil: JetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil
 96→
 97→    @Inject
 98→    internal lateinit var activityLauncherWrapper: ActivityLauncherWrapper
 99→
100→    private val backPressedCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(true) {
101→        override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
102→            mainViewModel.onBackPressed()
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private val domainCheckoutActivityLauncher = registerForActivityResult(OpenCheckout()) {
107→        mainViewModel.onCheckoutResult(it)
108→    }
109→
110→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
111→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
112→        setContentView(R.layout.site_creation_activity)
113→
114→        onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backPressedCallback)
115→
116→        mainViewModel.start(savedInstanceState, getSiteCreationSource())
117→        mainViewModel.preloadThumbnails(this)
118→
119→        observeVMState()
120→        observeOverlayEvents(savedInstanceState)
121→    }
122→
123→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
124→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
125→        mainViewModel.writeToBundle(outState)
126→    }
127→
128→    @Suppress("LongMethod")
129→    private fun observeVMState() {
130→        mainViewModel.navigationTargetObservable.observe(this, ::showStep)
131→        mainViewModel.onCompleted.observe(this) { (result, isTitleTaskComplete) ->
132→            val intent = Intent().apply {
133→                putExtra(SitePickerActivity.KEY_SITE_LOCAL_ID, (result as? Created)?.site?.id)
134→                putExtra(SitePickerActivity.KEY_SITE_TITLE_TASK_COMPLETED, isTitleTaskComplete)
135→                // Let `SitePickerActivity` handle this with a SnackBar message
136→                putExtra(SitePickerActivity.KEY_SITE_CREATED_BUT_NOT_FETCHED, result is CreatedButNotFetched)
137→            }
138→            setResult(if (result is Completed) Activity.RESULT_OK else Activity.RESULT_CANCELED, intent)
139→            finish()
140→        }
141→        mainViewModel.dialogActionObservable.observe(this) {
142→            it?.show(this, supportFragmentManager, uiHelpers)
143→        }
144→        mainViewModel.exitFlowObservable.observe(this) {
145→            setResult(Activity.RESULT_CANCELED)
146→            finish()
147→        }
148→        mainViewModel.onBackPressedObservable.observe(this) {
149→            ActivityUtils.hideKeyboard(this)
150→            onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressedCompat(backPressedCallback)
151→        }
152→        mainViewModel.showDomainCheckout.observe(this, domainCheckoutActivityLauncher::launch)
153→        siteCreationIntentsViewModel.onBackButtonPressed.observe(this) { mainViewModel.onBackPressed() }
154→        siteCreationIntentsViewModel.onSkipButtonPressed.observe(this) { mainViewModel.onSiteIntentSkipped() }
155→        siteCreationSiteNameViewModel.onBackButtonPressed.observe(this) {
156→            mainViewModel.onBackPressed()
157→            ActivityUtils.hideKeyboard(this)
158→        }
159→        siteCreationSiteNameViewModel.onSkipButtonPressed.observe(this) {
160→            ActivityUtils.hideKeyboard(this)
161→            mainViewModel.onSiteNameSkipped()
162→        }
163→        hppViewModel.onBackButtonPressed.observe(this) { mainViewModel.onBackPressed() }
164→        hppViewModel.onDesignActionPressed.observe(this) { mainViewModel.onDesignSelected(it.template) }
165→        progressViewModel.onCancelWizardClicked.observe(this) { mainViewModel.onWizardCancelled() }
166→        progressViewModel.onFreeSiteCreated.observe(this, mainViewModel::onFreeSiteCreated)
167→        progressViewModel.onCartCreated.observe(this, mainViewModel::onCartCreated)
168→        previewViewModel.onOkButtonClicked.observe(this, mainViewModel::onWizardFinished)
169→    }
170→
171→    private fun observeOverlayEvents(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
172→        if(BuildConfig.IS_JETPACK_APP)
173→            return
174→
175→        val fragment =  if (savedInstanceState == null) {
176→            JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayFragment
177→                .newInstance(
178→                    isSiteCreationOverlay = true,
179→                    siteCreationSource = getSiteCreationSource()
180→                )
181→        }else {
182→            supportFragmentManager.findFragmentByTag(JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayFragment.TAG)
183→                    as JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayFragment
184→        }
185→
186→        jetpackFullScreenViewModel.action.observe(this) { action ->
187→            if (mainViewModel.siteCreationDisabled) finish()
188→            when (action) {
189→                is OpenPlayStore -> {
190→                    fragment.dismiss()
191→                    activityLauncherWrapper.openPlayStoreLink(this, JETPACK_PACKAGE_NAME)
192→                }
193→                is DismissDialog -> {
194→                    fragment.dismiss()
195→                }
196→                else -> fragment.dismiss()
197→            }.exhaustive
198→        }
199→
200→        mainViewModel.showJetpackOverlay.observeEvent(this) {
201→            if (mainViewModel.siteCreationDisabled)
202→                showFragment(fragment, JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayFragment.TAG)
203→            else fragment.show(supportFragmentManager, JetpackFeatureFullScreenOverlayFragment.TAG)
204→        }
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getSiteCreationSource(): SiteCreationSource {
208→        val siteCreationSource = intent.extras?.getString(ARG_CREATE_SITE_SOURCE)
209→        return SiteCreationSource.fromString(siteCreationSource)
210→    }
211→
212→    override fun onIntentSelected(intent: String?) {
213→        mainViewModel.onSiteIntentSelected(intent)
214→        if (!siteNameFeatureConfig.isEnabled()) {
215→            ActivityUtils.hideKeyboard(this)
216→        }
217→    }
218→
219→    override fun onSiteNameEntered(siteName: String) {
220→        mainViewModel.onSiteNameEntered(siteName)
221→        ActivityUtils.hideKeyboard(this)
222→    }
223→
224→    override fun onDomainSelected(domain: DomainModel) {
225→        mainViewModel.onDomainsScreenFinished(domain)
226→    }
227→
228→    override fun onPlanSelected(plan: PlanModel) {
229→        mainViewModel.onPlanSelection(plan)
230→    }
231→
232→    override fun onHelpClicked(origin: Origin) {
233→        ActivityLauncher.viewHelp(this, origin, null, null)
234→    }
235→
236→    private fun showStep(target: WizardNavigationTarget<SiteCreationStep, SiteCreationState>) {
237→        val screenTitle = getScreenTitle(target.wizardStep)
238→        val fragment = when (target.wizardStep) {
239→            INTENTS -> SiteCreationIntentsFragment()
240→            SITE_NAME -> SiteCreationSiteNameFragment.newInstance(target.wizardState.siteIntent)
241→            SITE_DESIGNS -> {
242→                // Cancel preload job before displaying the theme picker.
243→                mainViewModel.preloadingJob?.cancel("Preload did not complete before theme picker was shown.")
244→                HomePagePickerFragment.newInstance(target.wizardState.siteIntent)
245→            }
246→            DOMAINS -> SiteCreationDomainsFragment.newInstance(screenTitle)
247→            PLANS -> SiteCreationPlansFragment.newInstance(target.wizardState)
248→            PROGRESS -> SiteCreationProgressFragment.newInstance(target.wizardState)
249→            SITE_PREVIEW -> SiteCreationPreviewFragment.newInstance(screenTitle, target.wizardState)
250→        }
251→        showFragment(fragment, target.wizardStep.toString(), target.wizardStep != SITE_PREVIEW)
252→    }
253→
254→    private fun getScreenTitle(step: SiteCreationStep): String {
255→        return when (val screenTitleData = mainViewModel.screenTitleForWizardStep(step)) {
256→            is ScreenTitleStepCount -> getString(
257→                screenTitleData.resId,
258→                screenTitleData.stepPosition,
259→                screenTitleData.stepsCount
260→            )
261→            is ScreenTitleGeneral -> getString(screenTitleData.resId)
262→            is ScreenTitleEmpty -> screenTitleData.screenTitle
263→        }
264→    }
265→
266→    private fun showFragment(fragment: Fragment, tag: String, slideIn: Boolean = true) {
267→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
268→            if (supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container) != null) {
269→                // add to back stack and animate all screen except of the first one
270→                addToBackStack(null)
271→                if (slideIn) {
272→                    setCustomAnimations(
273→                        R.anim.activity_slide_in_from_right, R.anim.activity_slide_out_to_left,
274→                        R.anim.activity_slide_in_from_left, R.anim.activity_slide_out_to_right
275→                    )
276→                } else {
277→                    setCustomAnimations(R.anim.fade_in, R.anim.fade_out, R.anim.fade_in, R.anim.fade_out)
278→                }
279→            }
280→            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment, tag)
281→        }
282→    }
283→
284→    override fun onPositiveClicked(instanceTag: String) {
285→        mainViewModel.onPositiveDialogButtonClicked(instanceTag)
286→    }
287→
288→    override fun onNegativeClicked(instanceTag: String) {
289→        mainViewModel.onNegativeDialogButtonClicked(instanceTag)
290→    }
291→
292→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
293→        if (item.itemId == AndroidR.id.home) {
294→            onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
295→            return true
296→        }
297→        return false
298→    }
299→
300→    companion object {
301→        const val ARG_CREATE_SITE_SOURCE = "ARG_CREATE_SITE_SOURCE"
302→        const val ARG_STATE = "ARG_SITE_CREATION_STATE"
303→    }
304→}

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.usecases
 2→
 3→import org.greenrobot.eventbus.Subscribe
 4→import org.greenrobot.eventbus.ThreadMode
 5→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
 6→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.generated.SiteActionBuilder
 7→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.NewSitePayload
 9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.OnNewSiteCreated
10→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.SiteVisibility
11→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.SiteVisibility.PUBLIC
12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceData
13→import org.wordpress.android.util.UrlUtilsWrapper
14→import javax.inject.Inject
15→import kotlin.coroutines.Continuation
16→import kotlin.coroutines.resume
17→import kotlin.coroutines.suspendCoroutine
18→
19→/**
20→ * Transforms OnNewSiteCreated EventBus event to a coroutine.
21→ */
22→class CreateSiteUseCase @Inject constructor(
23→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
24→    @Suppress("unused") private val siteStore: SiteStore,
25→    private val urlUtilsWrapper: UrlUtilsWrapper
26→) {
27→    private var continuation: Continuation<OnNewSiteCreated>? = null
28→
29→    @Suppress("UseCheckOrError")
30→    suspend fun createSite(
31→        siteData: SiteCreationServiceData,
32→        languageWordPressId: String,
33→        timeZoneId: String,
34→        siteVisibility: SiteVisibility = PUBLIC,
35→        dryRun: Boolean = false
36→    ): OnNewSiteCreated {
37→        if (continuation != null) {
38→            throw IllegalStateException("Create site request has already been sent.")
39→        }
40→        /*
41→         * To create a site with WordPress.com sub-domain, we need to pass the domain name without the "wordpress.com"
42→         * whereas to create a site with other domains, we need to pass the full url. This issue is addressed
43→         * in this use case since it's closest to the network layer.
44→         *
45→         * Ideally API wouldn't work like this or if it does FluxC is the one that handles the issue. However, at the
46→         * time of this comment, changing FluxC's Payload might end up affecting the old site creation flow,
47→         * so the workaround is applied here instead.
48→         */
49→        val domain = when {
50→            siteData.domain.isNullOrEmpty() -> null
51→            isWordPressComSubDomain(siteData.domain) -> urlUtilsWrapper.extractSubDomain(siteData.domain)
52→            else -> siteData.domain
53→        }
54→        return suspendCoroutine { cont ->
55→            val newSitePayload = NewSitePayload(
56→                domain,
57→                siteData.title,
58→                languageWordPressId,
59→                timeZoneId,
60→                siteVisibility,
61→                siteData.segmentId,
62→                siteData.siteDesign,
63→                dryRun,
64→                findAvailableUrl = if (siteData.isFree) null else true
65→            )
66→            continuation = cont
67→            dispatcher.dispatch(SiteActionBuilder.newCreateNewSiteAction(newSitePayload))
68→        }
69→    }
70→
71→    @Suppress("unused")
72→    @Subscribe(threadMode = ThreadMode.BACKGROUND)
73→    fun onNewSiteCreated(event: OnNewSiteCreated) {
74→        continuation?.resume(event)
75→        continuation = null
76→    }
77→}
78→
79→fun isWordPressComSubDomain(url: String) = url.endsWith(".wordpress.com")
80→

 53→
 54→@AndroidEntryPoint
 55→class SiteCreationPlansFragment : Fragment(), SiteCreationPlansWebViewClientListener {
 56→    private val viewModel: SiteCreationPlansViewModel by viewModels()
 57→
 58→    override fun onCreateView(
 59→        inflater: LayoutInflater, container: ViewGroup?,
 60→        savedInstanceState: Bundle?
 61→    ): View = ComposeView(requireContext()).apply {
 62→        setContent {
 63→            AppTheme {
 64→                SiteCreationPlansPage(
 65→                    navigationUp = requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher::onBackPressed
 66→                )
 67→            }
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 73→
 74→        viewModel.start(requireNotNull(requireArguments().getParcelableCompat(ARG_STATE)))
 75→        viewModel.actionEvents.onEach(this::handleActionEvents).launchIn(viewLifecycleOwner.lifecycleScope)
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun handleActionEvents(actionEvent: SiteCreationPlansActionEvent) {
 79→        when (actionEvent) {
 80→            is SiteCreationPlansActionEvent.CreateSite -> {
 81→                (requireActivity() as PlansScreenListener).onPlanSelected(actionEvent.planModel)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    // SiteCreationWebViewClient
 87→    override fun onPlanSelected(uri: Uri) {
 88→        viewModel.onPlanSelected(uri)
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    override fun onWebViewPageLoaded() {
 92→        viewModel.onUrlLoaded()
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    override fun onWebViewReceivedError() {
 96→        viewModel.onWebViewError()
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    @Composable
100→    @SuppressLint("UnusedMaterialScaffoldPaddingParameter")
101→    fun SiteCreationPlansPage(
102→        navigationUp: () -> Unit = { },
103→        viewModel: SiteCreationPlansViewModel = viewModel(),
104→    ) {
105→        val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
106→        Scaffold(
107→            topBar = {
108→                MainTopAppBar(
109→                    title = stringResource(R.string.site_creation_plans_selection_title),
110→                    navigationIcon = NavigationIcons.BackIcon,
111→                    onNavigationIconClick = navigationUp
112→                )
113→            },
114→            content = { SiteCreationPlansContent(uiState) }
115→        )
116→    }
117→
118→    @SuppressLint("SetJavaScriptEnabled")
119→    @Composable
120→    private fun SiteCreationPlansContent(uiState: SiteCreationPlansUiState) {
121→        when (uiState) {
122→            is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Preparing -> LoadingState()
123→            is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Prepared,
124→            is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Loaded -> SiteCreationPlansWebView(uiState)
125→            is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Error -> SiteCreationPlansError(uiState)
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    @Composable
130→    fun SiteCreationPlansError(error: SiteCreationPlansUiState.Error) {
131→        Column(
132→            horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally,
133→            verticalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
134→            modifier = Modifier
135→                .padding(20.dp)
136→                .fillMaxWidth()
137→                .fillMaxHeight(),
138→        ) {
139→            Text(
140→                text = uiStringText(uiString = error.title),
141→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.h5,
142→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center
143→            )
144→            Text(
145→                text = uiStringText(uiString = error.description),
146→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.body1,
147→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
148→                modifier = Modifier.padding(top = 8.dp)
149→            )
150→            if (error.button != null) {
151→                Button(
152→                    modifier = Modifier.padding(top = 8.dp),
153→                    onClick = error.button.click
154→                ) {
155→                    Text(text = uiStringText(uiString = error.button.text))
156→                }
157→            }
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    @SuppressLint("SetJavaScriptEnabled")
162→    @Composable
163→    private fun SiteCreationPlansWebView(uiState: SiteCreationPlansUiState) {
164→        var webView: WebView? by remember { mutableStateOf(null) }
165→
166→        if (uiState is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Prepared) {
167→            val model = uiState.model
168→            LaunchedEffect(true) {
169→                webView = WebView(requireContext()).apply {
170→                    layoutParams = ViewGroup.LayoutParams(
171→                        ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
172→                        ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
173→                    )
174→                    scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_INSIDE_OVERLAY
175→                    settings.userAgentString = model.userAgent
176→                    settings.javaScriptEnabled = model.enableJavascript
177→                    settings.domStorageEnabled = model.enableDomStorage
178→                    webViewClient = SiteCreationPlansWebViewClient(this@SiteCreationPlansFragment)
179→                    postUrl(WPWebViewActivity.WPCOM_LOGIN_URL, model.addressToLoad.toByteArray())
180→                }
181→            }
182→        }
183→
184→        Box(
185→            modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
186→            contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
187→        ) {
188→            if (uiState is SiteCreationPlansUiState.Prepared) {
189→                LoadingState()
190→            } else {
191→                webView?.let { theWebView ->
192→                    AndroidView(
193→                        factory = { theWebView },
194→                        modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()
195→                    )
196→                }
197→            }
198→        }
199→    }
200→
201→    companion object {
202→        const val TAG = "site_creation_plans_fragment_tag"
203→
204→        fun newInstance(siteCreationState: SiteCreationState) = SiteCreationPlansFragment().apply {
205→            arguments = Bundle().apply {
206→                putParcelable(ARG_STATE, siteCreationState)
207→            }
208→        }
209→    }
210→}

By Joshua N. Miller

In 2019, Marvel Comics was getting ready to celebrate its 80th anniversary. It had begun as Timely Comics, and quickly came to dominate the landscape with superheroes like the Human Torch and Namor the Submariner; now, for a special graphic-novel collection, the publisher invited superstar writer Art Spiegelman to write an introduction reflecting on Marvel’s legacy. There was just one issue: They asked him to compromise his own legacy to do so.

Spiegelman is best known for his independent graphic novel Maus, which retold his father’s experience surviving the Holocaust. Yet, when he compared then-president Donald Trump to the Red Skull, the fascist supervillain leader of a Nazi organization known as Hydra, Marvel balked. Demanding that a Jewish writer revise his material for the sake of remaining apolitical felt particularly disingenuous, given that Captain America made his 1941 debut punching Hitler in the face on the cover of his first issue. Over the years, Marvel writers had used superheroes to explore myriad political and social issues — sometimes explicitly, sometimes by merely existing. The success of 2018’s Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, and the Spidersona trend that followed it, attests to the positive impact diverse representation can have on audiences.

Superhero comics aren’t only political, but they’ve nearly always been political. Since Superman’s debut first made the genre mainstream, superheroes and their stories have advocated for social justice. And while some writers (and the corporations that employ them) may have lost sight of this reality, the communities these heroes represent can see when the forces that be abdicate the responsibility the heroes gladly shoulder. The essays collected below explore how superhero comics capture the lived experiences of marginalized communities — as well as how some comics have only served to stigmatize them.

Golden Age Superheroes Were Shaped By the Rise of Fascism (Art Spiegelman, The Guardian, August 2019)

In recalling the origins of the superhero genre, Spiegelman stresses that the immaturity often ascribed to comic books was as prevalent in the 1930s as it is in the present. If there’s any notable difference, it likely lies in the fact that publishers themselves were aware of this and consequently chose not to prioritize making the high-quality products we see today. (“Just give them a lot of action and don’t use too many words,” publisher Martin Goodman once told Stan Lee.) Though Superman’s debut shifted the medium by introducing an archetype that captured the hearts (and dimes) of millions of readers, the sentiment that comic books were for immature audiences endured. But the adventures of these heroes and the lives of the creators who made them tell a different tale.

Bold as it was to introduce Captain America by having him punch Hitler on the cover of his first issue in 1941, the impact of his debut is better appreciated if you understand how tolerant the U.S. was toward Nazi sympathizers until the country properly entered World War II. Just two years prior to Cap’s debut, thousands of members of the German American Bund rallied in New York to express their approval of Hitler’s antisemitic sentiments. The sales of superhero comics began to dwindle after the war, which not only suggests that consumers were aware of their political nature, but implies that publishers were willing to exploit those politics for profit.

The pioneers behind this embryonic medium based in New York were predominantly Jewish and from ethnic minority backgrounds. It wasn’t just Siegel and Shuster, but a whole generation of recent immigrants and their children – those most vulnerable to the ravages of the great depression – who were especially attuned to the rise of virulent antisemitism in Germany. They created the American Übermenschen who fought for a nation that would at least nominally welcome “your tired, your poor, your huddled masses yearning to breathe free.”

Superman is Jewish: The Hebrew Roots of America’s Greatest Superhero (Rich Goldstein, The Daily Beast, July 2017)

Much of Michael Chabon’s The Adventures of Kavalier & Klay is based on the lives of Jerry Siegel, Joe Shuster, Stan Lee, and other comic-book creators.

Straight to the point, Goldstein opens his essay by making an important observation: Superman owes his stranger-in-a-strange-land origin to the Jewish heritage of his creators, Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster. But his roots are often overshadowed by his association with American culture. Contemporary comics frequently depict Superman with American iconography, even though he’s repeatedly acted as a hero for Earth and beyond. So it’s not surprising to think that readers would try to compare him to Jesus Christ, given the important role that Christianity plays within traditional American values. Yet, hyper-fixating on Clark’s Christlike attributes serves to obfuscate the superhero’s Jewish origins.

Here was a being who had immigrated to a land that supposedly offered the prospects of a better life, and changed his name (Kal-El to Clark Kent) to do so  — just as some Jewish families who immigrated to the U.S. changed their names to avoid prejudice. Goldstein posits that the antisemitic sentiments of the time would have gotten Superman’s comic cancelled before it even began, and invites the reader to question how warmly the character would have been received if his Jewish influences were more explicit. More importantly, however, his article affirms the fact that the creation of Superman — and the rise of the superhero genre — should be credited to the imaginations of Jewish creators who produced a hero that stood against the oppression they faced.

Superman is as American as apple pie, in that both have their origins in the Middle East. Apples, because they are thought to have been first domesticated in Turkey, and Superman, because of his oftentimes overlooked Jewish heritage.

House of X / Power of X Moves Mutants Forward (Stephanie Williams, SYFY, January 2020)

Even those largely unfamiliar with superhero comics are likely aware of the X-Men. But before the blockbuster sales and long-running film franchise, the beloved mutants came to prominence thanks to the efforts of Len Wein, who created characters like Storm and Wolverine, and Chris Claremont, who confronted them with dilemmas that blurred the traditional lines of good and evil. At the heart of the franchise’s moral ambiguity is the ideological schism between the X-Men founder Charles Xavier (Professor X), who seeks to protect mutants and mankind despite the latter’s extreme prejudices against the former, and Erik Lensherr (Magneto), who prioritizes the well-being of mutants above all else. The duo has often been compared to Martin Luther King Jr. and Malcolm X for the characters’ respective beliefs, but since the X-Men were created any significant strides toward the dreams of either camp have been brief — if they existed at all. 

That changed in 2019 with the debut of House of X / Powers of X. Spearheaded by creators Jonathan Hickman, Pepe Larraz, and R.B. Silva, HOXPOX offered mutants a way forward that appeased both Xavier’s accomodationism and Lensherr’s hardline approach: separation. The first issue lays out a broad-based mutant-led effort to create a culture detached from mankind. As Williams notes in her review, part of the appeal of the comic’s premise lies in the fact that mutants finally “get out of their own way”; recognizing how paralyzed they’ve been, they acknowledge that the only way to advance is to de-center the desires of the community that so despised them. Three years after their new culture’s founding, the mutants still face opponents bent on their destruction. The difference now is that extinction no longer lurks in every defeat. On the contrary, every victory against those who seek to destroy them asserts that they, and the marginalized communities they’ve been thought to represent, owe nothing to their oppressors when it comes to their survival.

The safety and future for all mutants becomes the only goal, and there is no trying to continue to convince humans they are worthy of occupying the same space. It’s proven time and time again to be lethal to incorporate humans into their plans. The focus is now Krakoa and making sure it remains a place where mutants can truly be free — all mutants, not just the ones who adhere to a specific ideology. There are still mutants who lose their lives in order to secure the future of Krakoa, but at least it’s for the greater good and not for selfish men.

‘House of M’ and the Vilification of Mental Illness (Lia Williamson, AIPT Comics, August 2021)

While Wanda Maximoff’s popularity has certainly been bolstered by her appearances in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, her character has a deep history within the Marvel comics. Introduced in the first issues of X-Men, Wanda (Scarlet Witch) was originally a villain operating with her brother Pietro (Quicksilver) as a member of Magneto’s Brotherhood of Evil Mutants. Over the decades, however, her allegiances changed, and she became a prominent member of the Avengers. Like most other characters, she’s gone through her fair share of ups and downs over the course of the past few decades, but none quite compare to the hardships she faced during the 2005 event House of M.

Most Marvel comics fans recall the event as the time when Wanda experiences a mental breakdown and loses control of her reality-changing powers. The consequences for these episodes are dire: She’s manipulated into creating an alternate reality where mutants aren’t hated, and subsequently depowers nearly all mutants when that reality is compromised. While all superhero parties involved were aware of Wanda’s poor mental state and how she was manipulated, since the comic’s release she has shouldered most of the blame for the tragedy. By dissecting the story, Williamson opens a dialogue regarding the treatment of the mentally ill in comics — and concludes that, much like Professor X and Magneto’s ideological conflict, there is rarely an effort made to properly address the issues. House of M failed Wanda on multiple levels: narratively, because it suggested that for all the crises the world’s mightiest heroes faced, they were helpless to support one of their own; and conceptually, because it places the onus of the fallout on a character who was robbed of her agency.

House of M is a low point for Wanda and how comics deal with the topic of mental illness overall, vilifying a woman’s mental breakdown by having her virtually wipe out a species (one she also belonged to in 2005, mind you) and kill several of her own teammates. The story strips her of her own agency while making her the sole bad guy left to pick up the pieces years later. It’s a story that says the mentally ill are dangerous, that we’re capable of horrible things and maybe we should be “put down” before those things can happen.

On Queer Superhero A-Listers and a Lack of Visibility (Madeleine Chan, AIPT Comics, June 2022)

One of the most notable examples of comic book media to emerge in the pandemic has been the Cerebro podcast. Hosted by literary agent Connor Goldsmith, each episode welcomes a guest to talk about the many characters of the X-Men franchise. These discussions often entail a discussion of the queer subtext at work throughout the characters’ histories (since explicit queerness was traditionally taboo in mainstream comics). In the past decade, one of the benefits of the increased push for diverse representation in comics has been increased visibility for queer characters. As Chan notes in this piece, both major and minor characters have been confirmed as queer either by their creators or within their comics — which raises the question of why there’s been so little effort to do the same for the major characters in their film and television incarnations.

But our world is no utopia, and so Chan’s essay raises the issue of how exactly queer characters are being portrayed in their respective comics. They note the cancellation of Al Ewing’s Guardians of the Galaxy run, which is interesting not only because it marked the second cancellation of a series in which Star-Lord was queer, but because the Guardians’ handling in the comics has been heavily impacted by their portrayal in their movies. With this essay, Chan has exposed yet another display of disingenuity in regard to Marvel and DC’s handling of marginalized characters. Their halfhearted approach to depicting queer characters suggests that their priority is to avoid upsetting intolerant audiences. 

While there is very limited visibility — Zoë Kravitz’s Catwoman in The Batman was played as bisexual and Ryan Reynolds’ Deadpool could be interpreted as pansexual — they still weren’t seen as queer by the larger public. And the queer hints in Wonder Woman 1984 were only supposed to be just that, and the odds for Star-Lord to be visible as queer in his upcoming MCU movies is low for multiple reasons. It seems DC and Marvel don’t want to risk disrupting the ubiquitously popular mainstream image of these characters to not deter their straight, cisgender male audience. Translations to the screen would be more likely if their queerness was more prominent in the source material.

Monica Rambeau Was WandaVision‘s Real Hero, and the Show Did Her Dirty (Charles Pulliam-Moore, Gizmodo, March 2021)

Many heroes have taken up the mantle of Captain Marvel in the Marvel universe. In the 2019 film about the character, we were introduced to two of them: Carol Danvers, who became closely associated with the role since she took it on in 2012, and Monica Rambeau. If the movie was the extent of your exposure to either character, you might assume that Monica wouldn’t follow in Carol’s footsteps when it came to being a superhero. And you’d have good reason not to, because Monica was actually Carol’s predecessor. Introduced as the second Captain Marvel in the same year the original died, she quickly rose to prominence as an Avenger, even becoming the team leader during Roger Stern’s tenure on the book. However, she was sidelined soon after losing her powers and relinquishing her title to a newer character that was made to have stronger ties to the original Captain Marvel. 

Since then, there have been various periods in the comics when Monica struggled to stay afloat appearance-wise. The past decade has seen a renewed effort to return her to the spotlight in titles like Ultimates, Damage Control, and her upcoming solo series. Unfortunately, her appearances in the Marvel Cinematic Universe so far have failed to capture her might in more ways than one. In this piece, Pulliam-Moore delves into the issues with Monica’s portrayal in her second MCU appearance, 2021’s WandaVision. It’s bad enough that Monica has been reduced to playing second fiddle as often as she has, but it’s much more problematic that she’s been portrayed in a way that suggests her heroic nature is heavily influenced by her white peers.

While it’s very nice to think that one would choose to be the bigger person after being possessed and attacked by a witch, this characterization for Monica given everything else that happens to her in the series felt like an unnecessary misstep that cast an unignorable shadow over the series as a whole. By not sticking its landing, all of WandaVision’s subtle sleights against Monica lose their ability to work as dynamic texture to the story and more like a reminder of how the show missed a choice opportunity to point out how Black sitcoms were huge for a hot second before disappearing almost entirely from network television.

‘Into the Spider-Verse’ and the Importance of a Biracial Spider-Man (Richard Newby, The Hollywood Reporter, December 2018)

When Stan Lee was asked why Spider-Man (Peter Parker) was one of his favorite characters, he responded that a key factor was Spider-Man’s relatability. “He’s the one who’s most like me,” he said in an interview with the Chicago Tribune, “he’s got a lot of problems, and he does things wrong and I can relate to that.” This sentiment is likely one that resonated with fans: Spider-Man became one of Marvel’s most popular characters shortly after his 1962 debut. Readers could see themselves in Peter Parker’s everyday struggles when he wasn’t wearing his mask. However, for legions of nonwhite readers, that relatability was only partial — at least until Miles Morales came along in 2011 ... [14 lines truncated] ...
